sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports back pain for approximately one week, initially sharp and recurring, exacerbated by sudden movements. He denies a clear injury and reports trying over-the-counter medications without relief. The pain is described as being more prominent on the lower right side and less so on the left, and occasionally radiating to the left eye. He also reports occasional left-sided pain with movement. He reports that his blood sugars have been trending towards the higher side and that he tests them most mornings. He admits to occasional visits to the bakery, but tries to limit his intake.
medical_concepts: radiating pain to the left eye

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals tenderness to palpation over the lumbar spine, particularly at the central lower region. Neurological exam is unremarkable, with no sensory deficits or motor weakness. Lung sounds are clear bilaterally. Heart rhythm is regular at 72 bpm. Blood pressure is 118/72. Blood sugar readings have been trending towards the higher side.
medical_concepts: tenderness to palpation over the lumbar spine

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's primary complaint is musculoskeletal low back pain, likely a strain. His history of diabetes is relevant to overall management. Further investigation is warranted to rule out other causes of the pain.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am ordering a low back x-ray for outpatient evaluation. I am also ordering physical therapy twice weekly to address the pain and strengthen back muscles. I am prescribing 400 mg ibuprofen as needed with tylenol up to three times daily. I am prescribing flexeril at night. I recommend monitoring blood sugars in the mornings and obtaining an A1C test. I will consider referral to a pain specialist if symptoms persist despite conservative management. I will follow up in four weeks to assess progress.

medical_concepts: radiating pain to the left eye
tenderness to palpation over the lumbar spine
